deductions.reason: In all three transcripts, when students successfully produce a defensible outline or draft after iterative refinement, the tutor moves directly to stress-testing or next steps without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the revised approach worked better than earlier attempts.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: consistently using diagnostic questions, refusing to give answers directly, pushing students to test their own claims with evidence, and scaffolding from rough actor lists to defensible structured outlines. No near-submission-ready content is provided; the tutor explicitly declines to write for students. Scaffolding builds well on student progress in each turn. The one consistent gap is the absence of reflection prompts after students successfully improve—the tutor moves to stress-testing or next steps without asking students to articulate what changed in their thinking. Meta-learning rationale is provided at least once per conversation (e.g., 'better to be narrow and defensible than broad and weak'; 'don't force a project name from memory'). Communication is concise and well-paced throughout.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: No deductions warranted. The tutor never provides final answers, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks students to generate their own reasoning before offering any framing. Key ideas remain student-led across all three conversations. 1.2: No deductions warranted. The tutor consistently builds on correct student steps and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3: A 2-point deduction applies under 1.3.C.a. In all three transcripts, when students successfully produce improved outlines after iterative work (Transcript 1 Turn 10, Transcript 2 Turn 10, Transcript 3 Turn 10), the tutor moves immediately to stress-testing the next weakness rather than pausing to ask what changed in the student's approach or why the revised framing worked. This is a consistent missed opportunity for process reflection. The 1.3.A and 1.3.B criteria are met: rationale for evidence-based reasoning is embedded throughout, and method-level feedback (e.g., 'sector can be enough if that's what your evidence shows'; 'don't force it in if you can't defend it') is specific and actionable. Dialogue quality and communication quality are strong with no deductions warranted.
